messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list copy command do?
The access-list copy command copiesan IPv4, IPv6, or MACACLtoanewdestinationACLoroverwritesanexistingACL. Syntax: access-list | {ip|IPv6|mac} | | <ACL-NAME> | copy | <DESTINATION-ACL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list copy command?
Syntax: access-list | {ip|IPv6|mac} | | <ACL-NAME> | copy | <DESTINATION-ACL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list log-timer command do?
The access-list log-timer command sets the log timer interval for all ACEs that have the log parameter configured. Syntax: access-list log-timer {default|<INTERVAL>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list log-timer command?
Syntax: access-list log-timer {default|<INTERVAL>}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list mac command do?
The access-list mac command is documented in this guide. Syntax: access-list mac <ACL-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list mac command?
Syntax: access-list mac <ACL-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list resequence command do?
The access-list resequence command resequences the ACEsequencenumbersinanACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list resequence command?
Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the access-list reset command do?

The access-list reset command changestheuser-specifiedACLconfigurationtomatchtheactiveACLconfiguration. Usethiscommand whenadiscrepancyexistsbetweenwhattheuserconfiguredandwhatisactiveandacceptedbythesystem. Syntax: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the access-list reset command?
Syntax: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip | IPv6 | mac} <ACL-NAME> {in | out}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip | IPv6 | mac} <ACL-NAME> {in | out}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command applies an ACL to the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Using the apply access-list command on an interface VLAN interface with an already-applied ACL of the same direction and type will replace the currently-applied ACL. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command?
Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
Syntax: clear access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show access-list control-plane command do?

The show access-list control-plane command shows information about your defined ACLs that have been applied to the Control Plane. When show access-list control-plane is entered without parameters, information for all ACLs applied to the Control Plane is shown. The parameters filter the list of ACLs for which information is shown. Available filtering includes:
- The content of a specific ACL that has been applied to the Control Plane.
- All ACLs of a specific type that have been applied to the Control Plane.
- All ACLs applied to the Control Plane for a specific VRF. Syntax: show access-list [ip|IPv6] [<ACL-NAME>] control-plane [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show access-list control-plane command?
Syntax: show access-list [ip|IPv6] [<ACL-NAME>] control-plane [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
Syntax: show access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>

The policy copy command copiesapolicytoanewdestinationpolicyoroverwritesanexistingpolicy. Copyingapolicycopiesallits entriesaswell. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> | copy | <DESTINATION-POLICY>.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the policy reset command do?

The policy reset command changes the user-specified policy configuration to match the active policy configuration. Use this command when a discrepancy exists between what the user configured and what is active and accepted by the system. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show boot-history command do?

The show boot-history command shows boot information. When no parameters are specified, shows the most recent information about the boot operation, and the three previous boot operations for the active management module. When the all parameter is specified, shows the boot information for the active management module and all available line modules. To view boot-history on the standby, the command must be sent on the standby console. Syntax: show boot-history [all].

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show needed-updates command do?

The show needed-updates command displays whether any programmable devices are in need of an update. Without the next-boot parameter, this command displays needed updates relative to the currently running AOS-CX image. With the next-boot parameter, this command displays needed updates relative to an AOS-CX image file in the persistent storage of the switch, which might be different from the currently running image. If either the primary or secondary parameter is specified, this command queries that specific AOS-CX image file. Otherwise, it queries the default AOS-CX image file as set by the most recent boot system or boot set- default command. Syntax: show needed-updates [next-boot [primary|secondary]].

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erase all zeroize command do?

The erase all zeroize command restores the switch to its factory default configuration. You will be prompted before the procedure starts. Once complete, the switch will restart from the primary image with factory default settings. Back up all data before running this command as all configuration settings will be lost. Syntax: erase all zeroize.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about About AOS-CX?

About AOS-CX: AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including:
- Automated visibility to help IT organizations scale: The Aruba Network Analytics Engine allows IT to monitor and troubleshoot network, system, application, and security-related issues easily through simple scripts. This engine comes with a built-in time series database that enables customers and developers to create software modules that allow historical troubleshooting, as well as analysis of historical trends to predict and avoid future problems due to scale, security, and performance bottlenecks.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the no shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the no ubt-client-vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no ubt-client-vlan <VLAN-ID> command specifies the UBT Client VLAN or local VLAN. This VLAN is used in local-VLAN mode only. If the UBT client VLAN is configured in VLAN-extend mode it is ignored. No other feature should be enabled on the UBT client VLAN. The no form of this command removes the VLAN to use for tunneled clients.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ubt-mode vlan-extend command do?

The ubt-mode vlan-extend command selects VLAN extended mode. When VLAN-extend mode is enabled clients are assigned to their UBT role- based VLAN in the hardware datapath. The no form of the command selects local-VLAN mode. In local-VLAN mode clients are assigned to a local switch VLAN and associated with their UBT role-based VLAN when client traffic reaches the controller. The default UBT mode is local-VLAN. Syntax: ubt-mode vlan-extend.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?

The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. Enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear lldp statistics command?
Syntax: clear lldp statistics
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show cdp command do?
The show cdp command shows CDP information for all interfaces. Syntax: show cdp.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show cdp command?
Syntax: show cdp
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show ztp information command do?
The show ztp information command shows information about Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations performed on the switch. Startson-demandZTP. Syntax: show ztp information.

Syntax: show ztp information

Syntax: show usb file-system | [<PATH>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show version command do?

The show version command shows version information aboutthenetworkoperatingsystemsoftware, serviceoperatingsystem software, andBIOS. AOS-CX10.07Fundamentals Guide Configures the polling interval for system resource information collection and recording such as CPU and memory usage. Shows CPU utilization information. Shows memory utilization information. Enables the USB ports on the switch. This setting is persistent across switch reboots and management module failovers. Both active and standby management modules are affected by this setting. The no form of this command disables the USB ports. Enables or disables the inserted USB drive. Syntax: show version.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is High Availability?

High Availability: Key goals of HA include:
- Achieve five-nines (99.999%) availability of switching traffic through minimization of unplanned network outages.
- Fault tolerant: No single active component failure will cause an outage.
- Live replacement of hardware with minimal or no disruption.
Terminology:
- MM: Abbreviation for management module - MM to MM link: Refers to the 10GbE-KR Ethernet link between two MMs - OVSDB: Abbreviation for Open vSwitch Database - Active MM: Management module that has control of the chassis - Standby MM: Backup management module for the active management module - JSON-RPC: Remote procedure call protocol encoded in JSON
Key parts of the HA feature include: Network redundancy: Protocols and redundant network paths provide redundancy in the network, enabling traffic to continue flowing if a network link or network switch fails. Hardware redundancy: Redundant hardware components (power supplies, fabric cards, management modules) allow continued switching traffic or system management in the event of a hardware failure or hardware maintenance.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is Management Module Failover?

Management Module Failover: There are two types of Management Module (MM) failover:
- Controlled failover: The user triggers this type of failover by rebooting the Active MM or running the redundancy switchover command.
- Uncontrolled failover: This type of failover is triggered by unexpected events like a crash on the Active MM or hot removal of the Active MM. In a dual MM chassis, the Standby MM detects failover events in one of the following ways:
- A mailbox interrupt is received from the Active MM to indicate takeover. This interrupt can come for controlled or uncontrolled failover (except for a hot removal).
- Active MM hot removal detection.
- Heartbeat loss detected on the Standby MM for more than 10 seconds. If the Active MM is not responding and is still not detected by the first two methods, it will be caught by this method.
Failover requirements:
- The Standby MM must be present to trigger a failover. An Unassigned MM will never trigger a failover.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Syntax: redundancy switchover.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the redundancy switchover command?
Syntax: redundancy switchover

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 Static Route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 Static Route

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd command do?

The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable. Syntax: bfd.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
Syntax: bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
Syntax: bfd all-interfaces
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?

The bfd detect-multiplier command sets BFD detection multiplier on an interface. The no form of this command sets the BFD detection multiplier to the default value of 5. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier | <MULTIPLIER>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier | <MULTIPLIER>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
Syntax: bfd disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
Syntax: bfd enable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd echo disable command do?

The bfd echo disable command disables support for BFD echo packets. Echo packet support is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables support for BFD echo packets. Toggling this feature on 8325 or 8360 switches may cause route flapping. Syntax: bfd echo disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
Syntax: bfd echo disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address | <IPv4-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?
The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: clear bfd | statistics | [session | <ID>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear bfd statistics command?
Syntax: clear bfd | statistics | [session | <ID>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd command?
Syntax: ip ospf | bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip route bfd command do?

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.

Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable

The show bfd command showsinformationforall BFD sessionsorforaspecific BFD session. Syntax: show bfd | [session | <ID>] | [all-vrfs | | vrf | <NAME>] | [vsx-peer].

Syntax: show hsc
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?

The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the clear erps statistics command do?
The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance. Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring command do?
The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi- instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command This command Specifies thesetof VLAN sthatareprotectedbythisringinstance. The no formofthiscommandremovesasetof VLAN sthatareprotectedbythisringinstance. Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command?
Syntax: erps | ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive

Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>

Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>

Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?

The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command?
Syntax: erps ring <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command the RPL owner node uses a delay timer before initiating an RPL block in case of both revertive mode of operation or before reverting to idle state after clearing operator commands (FS, MS). The Wait to Restore (WTR) timer can be configured in 1-minute increments up to 12 minutes. The default value is 5 minutes. When recovering from an SF, the delay timer must be long enough to allow the recovering network to become stable. In the default revertive mode of operation, the WTR timer is used to prevent frequent operation of protection switching due to intermittent SF defects. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the wtr-interval and sets it to the default value of 5 minutes. Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command?
Syntax: erps ring | <ringid>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps statistics command do?

The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports. Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps statistics command?
Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps status command do?

The show erps status command this command displays detailed information about a specific ring or all instances of a ring. The ring instance may be in one of the following states:
- Idle: The ring instance is operational.
- Initializing: The ring instance is not operational.
- Protection: Protection switching has been triggered by a local or remote link failure.
- Pending: Pending clearance of a previous protection switch.
- Down: Ring instance is not active.
- Manual-switch: Manual protection switching triggered by Admin-down.
- Force-switch: Forced protection switching triggered by admin. A ring instance has the following reasons for "down" state:
- Disabled: Ring instance is administratively disabled. Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps status command?
Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the show erps summary command do?
The show erps summary command thiscommanddisplaysasummaryof the ERPSconfigurationandstatefortheERPSringinstances. Syntax: show erps summary.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the show erps summary command?
Syntax: show erps summary
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you accessing the AOS-CX Web UI?

To accessing the AOS-CX Web UI, follow the documented procedure: 1. Start a supported web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar 2. At the login page, enter your user name and password credentials, then click Login 3. After you log in, the main Web UI page is displayed.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the VSX command do?

The VSX command shows information for PoE ports in the switch. Allows you to configure PoE for the ports. Shows the Aruba Virtual Switching Framework (VSF) configuration and status information. This link is displayed for the 6200 and 6300 switches that support VSF. Not applicable to the 6100 Switch Series.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, how do you add and deleting a VLAN?
To add and deleting a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the following parameters: 2. Click Delete VLAN.

The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and enters loopback configuration mode. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: interface loopback <INSTANCE>.

The ip route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip route distance command?
Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 route distance command do?

The IPv6 route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv6 static route The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.

Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>

The show IPv6 rib command shows the IP v6Routing Information Base(RIB)ofVRF withname(<VRF-NAME>). IfVRF nameisnot specified, default VRF routesaredisplayed. Syntax: show IPv6 | rib <FILTER> | [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

Syntax: show IPv6 | rib <FILTER> | [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?

The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead, to the default for the OSPF interface. The default value is 40 seconds (generally 4 times the hello packet interval). Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.

Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?
Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip irdp holdtime command do?

The ip irdp holdtime command specifies the maximum amount of time the host will consider an advertisement to be valid until a newer advertisement arrives. When a new advertisement arrives, hold time is reset. Hold time must be greater than or equal to the maximum advertisement interval. Therefore, if the hold time for an advertisement expires, the host can reasonably conclude that the router interface that sent the advertisement is no longer available. The default hold time is three times the maximum advertisement interval. Syntax: ip irdp holdtime <TIME>.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the no sflow command do?

The no sflow command defines the IP address of the sFlow agent to use in sFlow datagrams. This address must be defined for sFlow to function. HPE recommends that the address: n can uniquely identify the switch n is reachable by the sFlow collector n does not change with time The no form of this command deletes the IP address of the sFlow agent. This causes sFlow to stop working and no datagrams will be sent to the sFlow collector. Defines a collector to which the sFlow agent sends data. Up to three collectors can be defined. At least one collector should be defined, and it must be reachable from the switch for sFlow to work. Disables the sFlow agent, but retains any existing sFlow configuration settings. The settings become active if the sFlow agent is re-enabled. SetsthesFlowheadersizeinbytes. Syntax: sflow agent-ip <IP-ADDR>.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip dhcp command do?

The ip dhcp command enables the DHCP client on the management interface enabling the interface to automatically obtain an IP address from a DHCP server on the network. By default, the DHCP client is enabled. Enables DHCP relay support. DHCP relay is enabled by default. DHCP relay is not supported on the management interface. The no form of this command disables DHCP relay support. Enables the DHCP relay hop count increment feature, which causes the DHCP relay agent to increment the hop count in all relayed DHCP packets. Hop count is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the hop count increment feature. Configures the behavior of DHCP relay option 82. A DHCP relay agent can receive a message from another DHCP relay agent having option 82. Syntax: ip dhcp.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is the syntax of the ip dhcp command?
Syntax: ip dhcp
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the ip helper-address command do?

The ip helper-address command defines the address of a remote DHCP server or DHCP relay agent. Up to eight addresses can be defined. The DHCP agent forwards DHCP client requests to all defined servers. This command requires that you define a source IP address for DHCP relay with the command ip source- interface. The configured source IP on the VRF is used to forward DHCP packets to the server. A helper address cannot be defined on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command removes an IP helper address. Syntax: ip helper-address <IPv4-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Configuration consistency requirements?

Configuration consistency requirements:
- Configure at least one active mode aggregation in two devices.
- For a successful static aggregation, make sure the ports at both ends of each link are in the same aggregation state.
- For a successful dynamic aggregation, make sure the peer ports of the ports aggregated at one end are also aggregated, and that one of the ends is configured as "active". The two ends can automatically negotiate the aggregation state of each member port. Removing interfaces - Deleting an aggregate interface also deletes its aggregation group and causes all member ports to leave the aggregation group.
- When a member interface is removed from a LAG: - 6100, 6200, 6300, and 6400 switches: The interface goes to its default status of unshut. - 8320, 8325, 8360, or 8400 switches: The interface becomes disabled. Disabling an interface When an interface LAG is disabled with the shutdown command, all its members also become operationally down. Layer 2 aggregation groups All switches support static and dynamic layer 2 aggregation groups.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what restrictions or notes are documented for Guidelines and limitations?

For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations:
- For faster convergence of routed traffic over Smartlink ports, ip neighbor-flood must be enabled on respective SVI interfaces.
- Smartlink uses ERPS copp class for flush packets. Limitations:
- VSX, ISL and MCLAGs cannot be included in Smartlink groups.
- Switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled exclude Smartlink ports from STP.
- On switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled, loops involving Smartlink and STP are not detected.
- On switches with both Smartlink and ERPS enabled, loops involving Smartlink and ERPS are not detected.
- ERPS and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Loop Protect and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port.
- Multicast fast convergence is not supported.
- Uplink Failure Detection (UFD) is not supported.
- MIB and WebUI are not supported..

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what is Multicast?

Multicast: Multicast addressing allows one-to-many or many-to-many communication among hosts on a network. Typical applications of multicast communication include: audio and video streaming, desktop conferencing, collaborative computing, and similar applications. IGMP snooping (Internet Group Management Protocol controls) can be configured per-VLAN basis to reduce unnecessary bandwidth usage. In the factory default state (IGMP and IGMP snooping disabled), the switch simply floods all IP multicast traffic it receives on a given VLAN through all ports on that VLAN (except the port on which it received the traffic). This can result in significant and unnecessary bandwidth usage in networks where IP multicast traffic is a factor. Enabling IGMP allows the ports to detect IGMP queries and report packets and manage IP multicast traffic through the switch. IGMP will be configured on the hosts, and multicast traffic will be generated by one or more servers (inside or outside of the local network).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command configures the drop unknown mode. While MLD snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that initiate an MLD request for multicast. Drop unknown mode can be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (exclusive-vlan). The default configuration is vlan-shared. The no form of this command disables drop unknown mode on the switch. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping [drop-unknown [vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive]].

section: Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the guide say about Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API?

Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API: The Aruba 6100 Switch Series only supports the default VRF and has no management port. Therefore, references in this guide to other VRFs or the management port do no apply to the 6100 Switch Series. Configuration for these switches should be done over an SVI having a physical port with access to the SVI, since the physical ports in the 6100 are not routed. AOS-CX REST API Switches running the AOS-CX software are fully programmable with a REST (REpresentational State Transfer) API, allowing easy integration with other devices both on premises and in the cloud. This programmability- combined with the Aruba Network Analytics Engine-accelerates network administrator understanding of and response to network issues. The AOS-CX REST API is a web service that performs operations on switch resources using HTTPS POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE methods. The AOS-CX REST API enables programmatic access to the AOS-CX configuration and state database at the heart of the switch.

The show port-access captive-portal-profile command shows the configuration information for all captive portal profiles or a particular captive portal profile. Within the captive portal context, defines the captive portal URL. The no form of this command deletes the captive portal URL. Within the captive portal context, defines the captive portal URL hash key. When this command is entered without parameters, plaintext hash key prompting occurs upon pressing Enter. The entered hash key characters are masked with asterisks. The no form of this command deletes the captive portal URL hash key. Syntax: show port-access captive-portal-profile [name <PROFILE-NAME>].

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the aaa authentication port-access auth-precedence command do?

The aaa authentication port-access auth-precedence command configures the per port authentication precedence using the space separator. By default, 802.1X authentication (dot1x) takes a higher precedence than MAC authentication (mac-auth). The no form of the command resets the port access authentication precedence to the default, 802.1X authentication followed by MAC authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access auth-precedence [dot1x mac-auth | mac-auth dot1x].

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the aaa authentication port-access auth-priority command do?

The aaa authentication port-access auth-priority command configures the authentication priority using the space separator to specific interface. Default auth-priority with concurrent onboarding is 802.1x followed by MAC authentication. With authentication precedence, the default auth-priority follows the auth-precedence order. The no form of the command resets the port access authentication priority to the default, is same as the configured auth-precedence order. The authentication priority is useful in deployments where clients such as wireless access points (APs), IT- compliant-laptops or phones, or laptops without pre-loaded supplicant software must download the supplicant software or firmware patches before attempting 802.1X authentication. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access auth-priority [dot1x mac-auth | mac-auth dot1x].

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator cached-reauth command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows 802.1X reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users already authenticated retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eapol-timeout command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eapol-timeout command configure the period the switch waits for a response from a client before retransmitting an EAPOL PDU. AOS-CX 10.07 Security Guide (6200, 6300, 6400 Switch Series) 246 If the value is 0, the time period is calculated as per RFC 2988. As per RFC 2988 2.1: Before Round-Trip Time (RTT) measurement, set Retransmission Timeout (RTO) to 3 seconds for initial retransmission and then double the RTO to provide back off as per section 5.5. Limit the maximum RTO (RTOmax) to 20 seconds as per section 4.3 of RFC 3748. The no form of the command resets the timeout period to the default. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator eapol-timeout <EAPOL-TIMEOUT>.

section: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.07, what does the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests command do?

The aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests command configures the number of EAPOL requests to send to a supplicant that must time out before authentication fails and the authentication session ends. The no form of the command resets the maximum number of EAPOL requests to the default, 5. Configures the maximum number of retries that the switch attempts to authenticate a client on a port before marking the client as unauthenticated. The no form of the command resets the maximum number of retries to the default, 2. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator max-eapol-requests <MAX-EAPOL-REQUESTS>.
